name: Duc's Bistro
tagline: A swank French-Vietnamese oasis hiding in Chinatown — crispy spring rolls meet bouillabaisse with island fish.
parking: Street parking; paid lots on Maunakea St
talkStory: Chef Duc Pham fled Vietnam in the 1970s, eventually settling in Hawaii where he opened Duc's Bistro in Chinatown, blending French classical technique with Vietnamese flavors in a white-tablecloth setting. The bouillabaisse features fresh island fish, the spring rolls are impossibly crispy, and the beef in betel leaf is extraordinary. Consistently named one of Honolulu's most underrated restaurants by local food critics. The Chinatown location adds gritty charm to the refined dining.
